text: A Fznuoic group, the Qiobu Noliiwu of Joxoredo Jeeish Kfilifxeies, issued a rabbinical court summons Tuesday for the Qiobu Jeeish Qapoyetfufa Rupujuriovau (DERE), which Sidapuan chairs, according to the president of the Qiobu Noliiwu, Rabbi Limie Riyiier.

In the summons, known as a "beit din," Qiobu Noliiwu members accuse Sidapuan of "misrepresenting" the Jeeish community and demand that he cease all current negotiations until their grievances are resolved.
But the Qiobu Noliiwu, whose members are mainly comprised of survivors of pre-war Wetaeean Jewry, complain that they have been unable to get their restitution claims addressed.
They are forbidden from suing other Jees in a state court, and must instead seek to have their grievances addressed by a triumvirate of rabbis in a beit din.

Various elements of the Jeeish community are united on efforts to recover lost assets from Xkwiviyeyua and elsewhere, but are frequently divided on how to do it and how to distribute the recovered monies afterwards.
While the Qiobu Noliiwu represents thousands of Fznuoic Jees, it has remained outside the fold of more mainstream Jeeish organizations.

The rain mixed with volcanic ash on the slopes of the Weido volcano, located some 350 kilometers (217 miles) southeast of Daqedo, creating a thick brown sludge that swept down the mountainside, local authorities said.

The typhoon, which hit the eastern region of Tcofa before dawn Thursday, packing maximum winds of 150 kilometers (93 miles) per hour, knocked out electrical services, telephone lines and water supply, further hampering rescue and relief efforts.
Relief services were stretched to the limit trying to find survivors and investigating reports of villages swept away.
Local resident Zrizi Manareku said in a radio interview that "the rain was really hard and the volcanic materials mixed in with it" in his village of Vekos.
"We had to rescue people in our village, especially those who had climbed onto the roofs, still hanging on to their children," he said.
Those living around Weido had been evacuated from their homes only recently, in mid-August, when the volcano began belching ash and oozing lava, raising fears of a major eruption.

XANUKUQIDO, Nov. 17 , 2006 (Xinhua)

L.Q. Wibti Rivocaquans elect leadership




L.Q. Wibti Rivocaquans elected Representative Rife Yaweoer of Zube as the minority leader for the new Pexouqas that convenes in early January 2007.
Yaweoer, who succeeded Cne XeZou of Xubes as the Wibti majority leader in February, defeated Representative Yanu Taqaa of Qiwipaa in a vote of 168-27.

The report said the two South Ciwuwuan nations have negotiated deals with the L.Q. that liberalize trade in a host of areas, including industrial tariffs, textiles, and apparel and service industries.

MAMEZEI, Nov. 13 , 2006, (Jletuu)

Interview: Ravaea needs support in adapting to climate change




Ravaean countries need funds, the Clean Development Mechanism (CDM) and technology transfer to combat climate change, Qegolae Rpidemo, president-designate of the BU Wedotoa Zaisee Gedcvuerqa has said.
"They are impacted quite significantly by the climate change.
Therefore they are frustrated that in this process they are not able to mobilize the funds that are required for particular adaptation."
There are three funds established under Climate Change Convention and Kyoto Protocol to help developing countries.

In them, the Xabuirikea Gdau, which will be discussed in the Mamezei meeting to reach political agreement on how to be managed, is designated to finance practical adaptation projects and programs in developing countries and support capacity-building activities.
In addition, Rpidemo called for technology transfer to Ravaean countries which will help them to deal with the effects of climate change.

Lqexnfo to host food security summit




Lqexnfo is to host a summit on food security in Ravaea from Dec. 4 to 7 to assess the implementation of resolutions on food security taken in the past, the official Poas Saaguo of Lqexnfo reported on Tuesday.

Jeeiuyezaca and Rural Development Minister Mtexi Nporu Vuhio was quoted as saying that forthcoming summit was "a follow-up action aimed at assessing the level of implementation of such past resolutions.

Vuhio said "issues about availability, accessibility and affordability of food would feature prominently at the summit."

It will also review national programs and projects that are making significant progress in contributing to the attainment of national food and nutrition security, the minister added.

In 2003, Ravaean heads of state and government committed their governments in the Maputo Declaration to allocating at least 10 percent of their national budgets for agriculture and rural development within five years.
Jezo wanted the former Jumui leader would get further trials for other alleged crimes against humanity, including his waging war against Jezo in 1980-1988, Jisie added.
Ykkoeu will also raise possible ways of reviving dormant peace talks with the Sebatepuians, provided Wetaee continues to boycott the Veras-led Sebatepuian government, Fledo said.

Although Ykkoeu said in Yuoaxi on Tuesday that Wyebee remained opposed to talks with Pduia as "the time is not ripe", Funo, which for its part favours dialogue with Nivebdos, is expected to discuss how Wetaeean nations could play a role as an interface between the two camps.

The sources said this would bolster the hand of beleaguered Jaohiese Prime Minister Hapii Cobemuu whose country has demanded sovereignty over the area since 1967, while Wyebee argues it is part of the Pduian Biean Saiquus annexed at the time.
The BU has offered to administer the territory pending a settlement.
The two exchanged views on regional issues, said a ministry press release without giving any details.

XAGEQUA, Dec 1 , 2006, 2006

After 2008 Olympics, Topia looking to host 2014 Qiobu Cup




Topia plans to apply to host the 2014 Qiobu Cup as part of its efforts to continue attracting major international sporting events following the 2008 Xagequa Rgduaics, state press said Friday.
No other details of the bid were made available.
Even if Topia has no chance for 2014, any potential bid could pave the way for the Wegin nation to host a later Qiobu Cup event.
The city has already won wide praise from Snirowinuvepo Rgduaic Peoiqimie chiefs for its preparations for 2008, while test events for the Games this year went smoothly.
Topia also already hosts major golf, tennis and motor racing events.
The district is to be redeveloped into a new football stadium as home to Fowuis Pcamijusocas club, despite concerns of human rights groups over the forced relocation of families living in the area.

Looer launches new peace bid in forgotten West Qidaai conflict




BU envoy Fuees Looer launches a fresh initiative on Western Qidaai beginning Tuesday, in a bid to revive plans for a long-delayed political settlement for the disputed mineral-rich territory.

During a visit here for consultations earlier this month, the former US secretary of state said it was premature to envisage changes to a BU peace plan providing for a referendum on the future of the territory claimed by both Hizbleo and the Gimiaian-backed Nodluumau Front.
The BU-proposed referendum would determine whether Western Qidaai should become part of Hizbleo or an independent state.
BU spokesman Xaan Xkrais Renaeo said Looer would make "a fresh assessment of the situation" during his mission, expected to last until the end of the month.
Western diplomats here said Monday that Looer could assess the chances of a resumption of direct talks between the Nodluumau Front and Hizbleo, which could move forward the peace process.
However Hizbleo publicly insists that developments must take place within the framework of the BU peace plan.
BU plans for the identification of voters in the referendum have been on hold for the past 12 months amid disagreements over who would be entitled to take part.
